---
title: "From Nutella on toast to statistical models"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    df-print: paged
---

## Introduction

Imagine a piece of toast that’s come out of the toaster with a faint grid pattern pressed in by the rack. We’ll treat each little square in that pattern as a possible outcome in our sample space. Next, we spread 1 Kg of Nutella across the toast so that some squares get a thick coating and others barely any at all.

Wherever the Nutella is **thick**, the probability is **high**; where it’s **thin or absent**, the probability is **low** or **zero**. But taken together, the **total** Nutella spread must sum to 1 Kg.

In this notebook, we’ll model several probability distributions/stastistical models as different ways of smearing Nutella across the toast.

![](images/nutella-toast.webp)

Once we have our sample space and probability distribution, we can:

-   Calculate the probability of any proposition/event in the sample space, i.e., any conclusion given the premises/assumptions/evidence

-   Sample from it to generate data in accordance with the model

## Setup environment

```{r}
#| output: false
library(tidyverse)
library(ggformula)

theme_set(theme_minimal())

set.seed(666)
```

## Helper function

```{r}
plot_model <- function(pdf, n = NULL) {
  n_cells <- nrow(pdf)
  
  # Decide style logic based on total number of cells
  if (n_cells < 100) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.5
    text_size   <- 3
  } else if (n_cells < 200) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.3
    text_size   <- 2
  } else if (n_cells < 500) {
    line_color  <- "black"
    line_width  <- 0.2
    text_size   <- NA
  } else {
    line_color  <- NA
    line_width  <- 0
    text_size   <- NA
  }
  
  # If samples are requested, do not add text labels
  if (!is.null(n)) {
    text_size <- NA
  }
  
  # Determine number of distinct X and Y values for axis text sizing
  n_x <- length(unique(pdf$X))
  n_y <- length(unique(pdf$Y))
  
  axis_text_x_size <- if(n_x < 10) { 12 } else if(n_x < 20) { 10 } else if(n_x < 40) { 8 } else { 6 }
  axis_text_y_size <- if(n_y < 10) { 12 } else if(n_y < 20) { 10 } else if(n_y < 40) { 8 } else { 6 }
  
  max_x <- max(pdf$X)
  max_y <- max(pdf$Y)
  
  # Determine the fill scale parameters based on n_cells:
  # For small grids, the range of p is set higher (midpoint and limit are larger)
  # For larger grids, the scale is "compressed" to better reflect small p values.
  if (n_cells < 100) {
    mid_val   <- 0.25
    limit_val <- 1
  } else if (n_cells < 200) {
    mid_val   <- 0.125
    limit_val <- 0.5
  } else if (n_cells < 500) {
    mid_val   <- 0.0625
    limit_val <- 0.25
  } else {
    mid_val   <- 0.03125
    limit_val <- 0.125
  }
  
  fill_scale <- scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("white", "chocolate4", "black"),
    values = c(0, mid_val, 1),
    limits = c(0, limit_val),
    oob = scales::squish
  )
  
  x_scale <- scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_x))
  y_scale <- scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, max_y))
  base_theme <- theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = axis_text_x_size, margin = margin(t = 2, b = 0)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = axis_text_y_size, margin = margin(r = 2, l = 0))
  )
  
  # Base tile plot
  plt <- gf_tile(
    Y ~ X,
    data = pdf,
    fill = ~ p,
    color = line_color,
    linewidth = line_width
  ) %>%
    gf_refine(
      fill_scale,
      x_scale,
      y_scale,
      base_theme
    ) +
    expand_limits(x = c(0, max_x), y = c(0, max_y)) +
    coord_fixed(expand = FALSE)
  
  # Optionally add text labels if text_size is applicable
  if (!is.na(text_size)) {
    plt <- plt %>% gf_text(label = ~ sprintf("%0.3f", p), size = text_size, alpha = 0.75)
  }
  
  # Optionally overlay random sample points if n is provided
  if (!is.null(n)) {
    plt <- plt %>% gf_point(
      Y ~ X,
      data = pdf %>% slice_sample(n = n, weight_by = p, replace = TRUE),
      size = 4,
      color = "red2",
      alpha = 0.5,
      stroke = 0
    )
  }
  
  return(plt)
}
```

## Grid, model and sampling parameters

```{r}
sample.size <- 50
grid.size <- 10
b0 <- 3
b1 <- 1
s  <- 1
e  <- seq(-s, s)
```

## Create sample space with uniform probability distribution

```{r}
X <- seq(0, 10)
Y <- seq(0, 10)

ss <-
  expand_grid(X, Y) %>%
  mutate(Ω = paste0("⍵", 1:n())) %>%
  select(Ω, everything())

ss
```

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(p = 1 / n())
```

## Uniform spreading of Nutella on a toast

### Small toast

```{r}
#| fig.width: 3
#| fig.height: 3
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1), Y = c(0, 1)) %>%
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  plot_model()
```

### Medium toast

```{r}
#| fig.width: 4
#| fig.height: 4
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2), Y = c(0, 1, 2)) %>%
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  plot_model()
```

### Large toast

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(p = 1 / n()) %>%
  plot_model()
```

### Calculate joint probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(X = x, Y = y \mid M_0)$

### Calculate joint probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

$$P(X = 1, Y = 2 \mid M_0)$$

```{r}
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  filter(X == 1, Y == 2) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

### Calculate marginal probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

$$P(X = 1 \mid M_0)$$

```{r}
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  filter(X == 1) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

### Calculate marginal probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

$$P(Y = 2 \mid M_0)$$

```{r}
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  filter(Y == 2) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

### Calculate conditional probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

$$P(Y = 3 \mid X = 1)$$

### Calculate conditional probability

```{r}
#| fig.width: 5
#| fig.height: 5
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    M1 = (X == 1)
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y \mid X = 1)$

$$P(Y = 3 \mid X = 1)$$

```{r}
expand_grid(X = c(0, 1, 2, 3), Y = c(0, 1, 2, 3)) %>%
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    M1 = (X == 1)
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)
  ) %>%
  filter(Y == 3) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

## Linear models

### Uniform model

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with no error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M1 = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_1) & = p(x, y \mid y = 3 + x) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, M_L) \quad M_L: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x
\end{aligned}
$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M1 = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)
  ) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with uniform error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M2 = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M2, p / sum(p[M2]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \in [-1, 1]) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x - 1) \lor (y = 3 + x + 0) \lor (y = 3 + x + 1)) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid (y = 2 + x) \lor (y = 3 + x) \lor (y = 4 + x)) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_U}) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_U}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \in [-s, s]
\end{aligned}
$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M2 = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M2, p / sum(p[M2]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with uniform error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dunif(Y, min = b0 + b1 * X - s, max = b0 + b1 * X + s) # M0, M2
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p\bigl(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \sim \text{Uniform}(\mu = 0, \sigma = 1)\bigr) \\
                 & = p\bigl(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_\text{Uniform}}\bigr) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Uniform}}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \sim \text{Uniform}(\mu = 0, \sigma) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Uniform}}: y \sim \text{Uniform} \bigl(\mu = (\beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x), \sigma \bigr)
\end{aligned}
$$

```{r}
ss %>%
  mutate(
    p = dunif(Y, min = b0 + b1 * X - s, max = b0 + b1 * X + s) # M0, M2
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with normal error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = s) # M0, M3
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p\bigl(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \sim \text{Normal}(\mu = 0, \sigma = 1)\bigr) \\
                 & = p\bigl(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}\bigr) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \sim \text{Normal}(\mu = 0, \sigma) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}: y \sim \text{Normal}\bigl(\mu = (\beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x), \sigma \bigr)
\end{aligned}
$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = s) # M0, M3
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Linear model with Cauchy error (heavier tails than the normal)

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dcauchy(Y, location = b0 + b1 * X, scale = s) # M0, M4
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p\bigl(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \sim \text{Cauchy}(\mu = 0, \sigma = 1)\bigr) \\
                 & = p\bigl(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_\text{Cauchy}}\bigr) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Cauchy}}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \sim \text{Cauchy}(\mu = 0, \sigma) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Cauchy}}: y \sim \text{Cauchy} \bigl(\mu = (\beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x), \sigma \bigr)
\end{aligned}
$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dcauchy(Y, location = b0 + b1 * X, scale = s) # M0, M4
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Non-linear model with no error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M3 = Y == (b0 + round(sin(b1 * X)))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M3, p / sum(p[M3]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M3 = Y == (b0 + round(sin(b1 * X)))
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M3, p / sum(p[M3]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

### Non-linear model with normal error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + round(sin(b1 * X)), sd = s)
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + round(sin(b1 * X)), sd = s)
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model(n = sample.size)
```

## Conditional probability and statistical association

### Uniform model

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$p(x, y)$

$$P(Y = 4)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$$P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    X1 = (X == 1)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(X1, p / sum(p[X1]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$P(Y = 4)\quad = \quad P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1) \implies$ there is no statistical association between $X$ and $Y$ given the assumptions of the uniform model ($M_0$), i.e., $X$ and $Y$ are independent

### Linear model with no error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M1 = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_1) & = p(x, y \mid y = 3 + x) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, M_L) \quad M_L: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x
\end{aligned}
$$

$$P(Y = 4, M_1)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M1 = (Y == b0 + b1 * X)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M1, p / sum(p[M1]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$$P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1, M_1)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M1 = (Y == b0 + b1 * X),
    X1 = (X == 1)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(X1 & M1, p / sum(p[X1 & M1]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$P(Y = 4 \mid M_1)\quad \neq \quad P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1, M_1) \implies$ there is a statistical association between $X$ and $Y$ given the assumptions of the linear model with no error ($M_1$), i.e., $X$ and $Y$ are dependent

### Linear model with uniform error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M2 = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M2, p / sum(p[M2]), 0)) %>%
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \in [-1, 1]) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x - 1) \lor (y = 3 + x + 0) \lor (y = 3 + x + 1)) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid (y = 2 + x) \lor (y = 3 + x) \lor (y = 4 + x)) \\
                 & = p(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_U}) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_U}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \in [-s, s]
\end{aligned}
$$

$$P(Y = 4 \mid M_2)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M2 = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(M2, p / sum(p[M2]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$$P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1, M_2)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = 1 / n() # M0
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    M2 = Y %in% (b0 + b1 * X + e),
    X1 = (X == 1)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(X1 & M2, p / sum(p[X1 & M2]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$P(Y = 4 \mid M_2) \quad \neq \quad P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1, M_2) \implies$ there is a statistical association between $X$ and $Y$ given the assumptions of the linear model with uniform error ($M_2$), i.e., $X$ and $Y$ are dependent

### Linear model with normal error

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = s) # M0, M3
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  plot_model()
```

$$
\begin{aligned}
p(x, y \mid M_2) & = p\bigl(x, y \mid (y = 3 + x + e), e \sim \text{Normal}(\mu = 0, \sigma = 1)\bigr) \\
                 & = p\bigl(x, y \mid \beta_0 = 3, \beta_1 = 1, \sigma = 1, M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}\bigr) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}: y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x + e \quad e \sim \text{Normal}(\mu = 0, \sigma) \\
                 \\
                 & M_{L+E_\text{Normal}}: y \sim \text{Normal}\bigl(\mu = (\beta_0 + \beta_1 \cdot x), \sigma \bigr)
\end{aligned}
$$

$$P(Y = 4 \mid M_3)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = s) # M0, M3
  ) %>%
  mutate(p = p / sum(p)) %>% # normalize probability density to sum to 1
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

$$P(Y = 4 \mid X = 1, M_3)$$

```{r}
ss %>% 
  mutate(
    p = dnorm(Y, mean = b0 + b1 * X, sd = s) # M0, M3
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>% 
  mutate(
    X1 = (X == 1)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p = if_else(X1, p / sum(p[X1]), 0)) %>%
  filter(
    Y == 4) %>% 
  summarize(P = sum(p)) %>% pull(P)
```

## How to fit any dataset with a single parameter

<https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.12320>

> "*With four parameters I can fit an elephant and with five I can make him wiggle his trunk.*" from a discussion between physicists John von Neumann and Enrico Fermi

[Von Neumann's elephant](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Von_Neumann%27s_elephant)

$$f(x) = \sin^2 (2^{x\tau} \arcsin \sqrt{\alpha})$$

$\tau$ is a constant that controls the desired level of accuracy

```{r}
f <- function(x, tau, alpha) {
  angle <- 2^(x * tau) * asin(sqrt(alpha))
  sin(angle)^2
}

x <- seq(-1, 1, length.out = 1000)

alpha <- 0.5

y <- f(x, alpha = alpha, tau = 3)

tibble(x = x, y = y) %>%
  gf_point(y ~ x) %>%
  gf_labs(x = "x", y = "f(x)")
```

## Print environment

```{r}
sessioninfo::session_info()
```
